Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics

get_threshold.Rd

Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics

Usage

get_threshold(
  stats,
  percentile1 = 0.05,
  percentile2 = 0.95,
  log = T,
  print.plot = T
)

Arguments

stats

a numeric vector.

percentile1

the percentile to set as the lower bound for calculating a local minimum.

percentile2

the percentile to set as the upper bound for calculating a local minimum.

log

logical (default TRUE), whether to use the log of the input statistics before calculating a threshold; useful for calculating threshold for filtering spots based on number of transcripts detected per-spot.

print.plot

logical (default TRUE), whether to print a plot of approximated function with intervals (red) and threshold (blue) plotted.

Value

The local minimum of the approximated function between the percentiles set by percentile1 and percentile2. A plot of the approximated function will also be printed if print.plot == TRUE with the returned value and intervals plotted.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# To set a threshold for number of transcripts per spot to filter low quality spots:
seu_obj = scc_s1
nCount = seu_obj$nCount_Spatial
thres = get_threshold(nCount)
seu_obj_filtered = subset(seu_obj, nCount_Spatial > thres)
} # }
